impacted.recommendation: Migrate API Management services to Premium SKU to support Availability Zones
APIM should have private endpoints enabled
APIM should have diagnostic settings enabled
Key Vault should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Managed Disks with 'Unattached' state.
recommendations.recommendation: Managed Disks with 'Unattached' state

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure shared disks let you attach a disk to multiple VMs at once for deploying or migrating clustered applications, suitable only when a disk is shared among VM cluster members.
recommendations.recommendation: Shared disks should only be enabled in clustered servers

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure disks offers a zone-redundant storage (ZRS) option for workloads that need to be resilient to an entire zone being down. Due to the cross-zone data replication, ZRS disks have higher write latency when compared to the locally-redundant option (LRS), so make sure to benchmark your disks.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Azure Disks with Zone Redundant Storage for higher resiliency and availability

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Availability zones are used when adding multiple replicas to your search service. Each replica is assigned to a different zone in the region. If there are more replicas than zones, they are distributed as evenly as possible across the available zones.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable AZ support in AI Search by configuring multiple replicas to your search service

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure AI Search should disable public network access
recommendations.recommendation: Azure AI Search should disable public network access

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure AI Search should have private enpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Azure AI Search should have private enpoints enabled
Azure AI Search should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Availability Zones, within each Azure region, are tolerant to local failures, protecting applications and data against unlikely Datacenter failures by being physically separate.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy VMs across Availability Zones

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: While availability sets are not scheduled for immediate deprecation, they are planned to be deprecated in the future. Migrate workloads from VMs to VMSS Flex for deployment across zones or within the same zone across different fault domains (FDs) for better reliability.
recommendations.recommendation: Migrate VMs using availability sets to VMSS Flex

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: The maintenance configuration settings let users schedule and manage updates, making sure the updates or interruptions on the VM are performed within a planned timeframe.
recommendations.recommendation: Use maintenance configurations for the Dedicated and/or Isolated VM SKUs

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure is retiring unmanaged disks on September 30, 2025. Users should plan the migration to avoid disruptions and maintain service reliability.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Managed Disks for VM disks

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Perform a test failover to validate your BCDR strategy and ensure that your applications are functioning correctly in the target region without impacting your production environment. Test your Disaster Recovery plan periodically without any data loss or downtime, using test failovers.
recommendations.recommendation: Validate VM functionality with a Site Recovery test failover to check performance at target

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: When Accelerated Networking is enabled, the default Azure VNet interface in GuestOS is swapped for a Mellanox, and its driver comes from a 3rd party. Marketplace images have the latest Mellanox drivers, but post-deployment, updating the driver is the user's responsibility.
recommendations.recommendation: When AccelNet is enabled, you must manually update the GuestOS NIC driver

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Enable backups for your virtual machines with Azure Backup to secure and quickly recover your data. This service offers simple, secure, and cost-effective solutions for backing up and recovering data from the Microsoft Azure cloud.
recommendations.recommendation: Backup VMs with Azure Backup service

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Production VM workloads should be deployed on multiple VMs and grouped in a VMSS Flex instance to intelligently distribute across the platform, minimizing the impact of platform faults and updates.
recommendations.recommendation: Run production workloads on two or more VMs using VMSS Flex

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: VM Insights monitors VM and scale set performance, health, running processes, and dependencies. It enhances the predictability of application performance and availability by pinpointing performance bottlenecks and network issues, and it clarifies if problems are related to other dependencies.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable VM Insights

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Replicating Azure VMs via Site Recovery entails continuous, asynchronous disk replication to a target region. Recovery points are generated every few minutes, ensuring a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) in minutes.
recommendations.recommendation: Replicate VMs using Azure Site Recovery

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Accelerated networking enables SR-IOV to a VM, greatly improving its networking performance by bypassing the host from the data path, which reduces latency, jitter, and CPU utilization for demanding network workloads on supported VM types.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Accelerated Networking (AccelNet)

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Capacity Reservations ensure high availability for virtual machines by reserving compute capacity in advance within a specific region or availability zone. This guarantees that VMs will have the necessary resources during peak demand or maintenance events, enhancing reliability and uptime.
recommendations.recommendation: Reserve Compute Capacity for critical workloads

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: IP forwarding allows a virtual machine network interface to receive and send network traffic not destined for or originating from its assigned IP addresses.
recommendations.recommendation: IP Forwarding should only be enabled for Network Virtual Appliances

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: A-series VMs are tailored for entry-level workloads like development and testing, including use cases such as development and test servers, low traffic web servers, and small to medium databases.
recommendations.recommendation: Don't use A or B-Series VMs for production needing constant full CPU performance

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Compared to Standard HDD and SSD, Premium SSD, SSD v2, and Ultra Disks offer improved performance, configurability, and higher single-instance VM uptime SLAs. The lowest SLA of all disks on a VM applies, so it is best to use Premium or Ultra Disks for the highest uptime SLA.
recommendations.recommendation: Mission Critical Workloads should consider using Premium or Ultra Disks

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Checkpoint optimization for large model training is crucial for disaster recovery. It reduces training time, increases reliability, improves cost efficiency, enhances resource utilization, and supports scalability by saving model states periodically to resume training from the last saved point.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure checkpoints are used for AI training models

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Service should have private enpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Service should have private enpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: If your primary workspace is unavailable, switch to the secondary workspace to continue work. Azure Machine Learning doesn't auto-submit jobs to the secondary workspace during an outage. Update your code configuration to point to the new workspace resource.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy Azure Machine learning workspace in secondary region

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Create compute resources for training a Machine Learning model in selected regions. Ensure both regions have sufficient compute quota for dynamic scaling. Customers must configure HA across zones for attached compute resources like AKS, Azure Databricks, Container Instances.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure to create Machine Learning Compute resources in secondary region

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Avoid selecting NC and NC_Promo series Azure virtual machines for machine learning VM quotas and make sure to migrate to newer versions.
recommendations.recommendation: Avoid NC and NC_Promo series Azure VMs for machine learning quotas; migrate to newer versions

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Requests for additional Azure Machine Learning quota should be made through the Azure Machine Learning Studio instead of the subscription level in the Azure portal.
recommendations.recommendation: Make Azure Machine Learning quota requests through the Azure Machine Learning Studio

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Service should disable public network access
recommendations.recommendation: Service should disable public network access

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: When selecting regions for BCDR, ensure that both regions offer adequate compute quotas to meet your requirements in the same SKU. This ensures that you can failover to the secondary region without any issues.
recommendations.recommendation: Selecting regions for BCDR, ensure that both regions offer adequate compute quotas
Service should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: When choosing SKUs, opt for those that support longer terms and steer clear of any SKUs that are nearing retirement. This ensures that you can continue to use the SKU for a longer period of time.
recommendations.recommendation: Choose SKUs with longer terms and avoid those nearing retirement

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Ensure you have a recovery strategy defined. Check regional availability and paired regions. Machine Learning doesn't have auto failover. Therefore, you must design a strategy that encompasses the workspace and all its dependencies, such as Key Vault, Azure Storage, and Container Registry.
recommendations.recommendation: Plan for a multi-regional deployment of Azure Machine Learning and associated resources

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
ODAA Vm Clusters can be in a number of states during their lifecycle (Available, Failed, Maintenance In Progress, Provisioning, Terminated, Terminating and Updating).
Ensure that the ODAA clusters are in Available state under normal operations.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure ODAA clusters are in Available state under normal operations
PostgreSQL should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: PostgreSQL should enforce SSL
recommendations.recommendation: PostgreSQL should enforce SSL

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: PostgreSQL should enforce TLS >= 1.2
recommendations.recommendation: PostgreSQL should enforce TLS >= 1.2

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: PostgreSQL should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: PostgreSQL should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configure storage auto-grow to prevent the server from running out of storage and becoming read-only.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure storage auto-grow
PostgreSQL should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: PostgreSQL should have private access enabled
recommendations.recommendation: PostgreSQL should have private access enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Enable HA with zone redundancy on flexible server instances to deploy a standby replica in a different zone, offering automatic failover capability for improved reliability and disaster recovery.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable HA with zone redundancy

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Use custom maintenance schedule on flexible server instances to select a preferred time for service updates to be applied.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable custom maintenance schedule

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configure GRS to ensure that your database meets its availability and durability targets even in the face of failures or disasters.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure geo redundant backup storage

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configure one or more read replicas to ensure that your database meets its availability and durability targets even in the face of failures or disasters.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure one or more read replicas

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Redirect mode enables direct connectivity to the instance bypassing the local gateway component and resulting in improved latency and throughput. Redirect mode applies to the VNet-local endpoint only, while the public and private endpoint will always operate in Proxy connection mode.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Redirect connection type to accelerate application access

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure SQL Managed Instance offers built-in availability by deploying multiple replicas in the same zone. For higher availability, use a zone-redundant configuration that spreads replicas across three Azure availability zones, each with independent power, cooling, and networking.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable zone redundancy for Azure SQL Managed Instance to improve high availability and resiliency

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configuring zone redundancy option for backups copies your backup file synchronously across three Azure availability zones in the primary region. If Geo is selected, then it copies your data asynchronously three times to a single physical location in the paired secondary region.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Zone-redundant or Geo-zone-redundant Backup storage redundancy
Virtual WAN should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Web Pub Sub should have availability zones enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Web Pub Sub should have availability zones enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Web Pub Sub should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Web Pub Sub should have private endpoints enabled
Web Pub Sub should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Managed Grafana Standard tier is hosted on a dedicated set of VMs to provide redundancy. With zone redundancy enabled, VMs are spread across availability zones (AZ). Related resources are also configured for AZ. Zone redundancy can only be enabled when creating the Azure Managed Grafana instance.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable zone redundancy in Managed Grafana

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
Azure Managed Grafana should have public network access disabled
to improve security. Use private endpoints or other secure connectivity methods
instead.
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Managed Grafana should disable public network access

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
Azure Managed Grafana should have zone redundancy enabled to ensure
high availability. Zone redundancy provides automatic replication across availability
zones for better resilience.
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Managed Grafana should have availability zones enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: API Connections not related to any Logic App.
recommendations.recommendation: API Connections not related to any Logic App
Azure Databricks should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Databricks should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Databricks should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Databricks should have the Public IP disabled
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Databricks should have the Public IP disabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
ODAA Exadata infrastructures can be in a number of states during its lifecycle (Available, Failed, Maintenance In Progress, Provisioning, Terminated, Terminating and Updating).
Ensure that the infrastructure is in Available state under normal operations.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure ODAA infrastructure is in Available state under normal operations

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Classic alerts for Recovery Services vaults in Azure Backup will be retired on 31 March 2026.
recommendations.recommendation: Migrate from classic alerts to built-in Azure Monitor alerts for Azure Recovery Services Vaults

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Cross Region Restore enables the restoration of Azure VMs in a secondary, Azure paired region, facilitating drills for audit or compliance and allowing recovery of VMs or disks in the event of a primary region disaster. It is an opt-in feature available exclusively for GRS vaults.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Cross Region Restore for your GRS Recovery Services Vault

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: With soft delete, if backup data is deleted, the backup data is retained for 14 additional days, allowing the recovery of that backup item with no data loss with no cost to you. Soft delete is enabled by default. Disabling this feature isn't recommended.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Soft Delete for Recovery Services Vaults in Azure Backup

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Deploying active-active VPN concentrators leverages the High Availability capabilities of the VPN Gateway on Virtual Hubs using a fully-meshed topology with four IPSec tunnels.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure diverse VPN Site links to different VPN concentrators on-premises

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Ensure sufficient compute resources to avoid host resource exhaustion in Azure VMware Solution, which utilizes vSphere DRS and HA for dynamic workload resource management. However, sustained CPU utilization over 95% may increase CPU Ready times, impacting workloads.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor CPU Utilization to ensure sufficient resources for workloads

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: For Azure VMware Solution, enabling Stretched Clusters offers 99.99% SLA, synchronous storage replication (RPO=0), and spreads vSAN datastore across two AZs. Must be done at initial setup, needing double quota due to extension across AZs.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Stretched Clusters for Multi-AZ Availability of the vSAN Datastore

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Ensure sufficient memory resources to prevent host resource exhaustion in Azure VMware Solution. It uses vSphere DRS and vSphere HA for dynamic workload management. Yet, continuous memory use over 95% leads to disk swapping, affecting workloads.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor Memory Utilization to ensure sufficient resources for workloads

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Ensure VMware vSAN datastore slack space is maintained for SLA by monitoring storage utilization and setting alerts at 70% and 75% utilization to allow for capacity planning. To expand, add hosts or external storage like Azure Elastic SAN, Azure NetApp Files, if CPU and RAM requirements are met.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure Azure Monitor Alert warning thresholds for vSAN datastore utilization

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: In regions with Availability Zones, assigning a zone-redundant frontend IP to a Standard Load Balancer ensures continuous traffic distribution even if one availability zone fails, provided other healthy zones and backend instances are available to receive the traffic.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure Standard Load Balancer is zone-redundant

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Selecting Standard SKU Load Balancer enhances reliability through availability zones and zone resiliency, ensuring deployments withstand zone and region failures. Unlike Basic, it supports global load balancing and offers an SLA.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Standard Load Balancer SKU

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Deploying Azure Load Balancers with at least two instances in the backend prevents a single point of failure and supports scalability. Pairing with Virtual Machine Scale Sets is advised for optimal scale building.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure the Backend Pool contains at least two instances
Load Balancer should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Outbound rules for Standard Public Load Balancer involve manual port allocation for backend pools, limiting scalability and risk of SNAT port exhaustion. NAT Gateway is recommended for its dynamic scaling and secure internet connectivity.
recommendations.recommendation: Use NAT Gateway instead of Outbound Rules for Production Workloads

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Load Balancers with empty backend address pools.
recommendations.recommendation: Load Balancers with empty backend address pools

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Network Interfaces not attached to any resource.
recommendations.recommendation: Network Interfaces not attached to any resource

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Create Alerts with Azure Monitor for operations like Create or Update Route Table to spot unauthorized/undesired changes in production resources. This setup aids in identifying improper routing changes, including efforts to evade firewalls or access resources from outside.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor changes in Route Tables with Azure Monitor

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Route Tables not attached to any subnet.
recommendations.recommendation: Route Tables not attached to any subnet

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: A Citrix Managed Azure subscription supports VMs with VDA for app/desktop delivery, excluding other machines like Cloud Connectors. When close to the limit, signaled by a dashboard notification, and with sufficient licenses, request another subscription. Can't exceed the given limits for catalogs.
recommendations.recommendation: Do not create more than 4000 Citrix VDA servers per subscription

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Service health gives a personalized health view of Azure services and regions used, offering the best place for notifications on outages, planned maintenance, and health advisories by knowing the services used.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure Service Health Alerts

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Subnets without Connected Devices or Delegation.
recommendations.recommendation: Subnets without Connected Devices or Delegation
MariaDB should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: The cluster auto-scaler in AKS adjusts node counts based on pod resource needs and available capacity, enabling scaling as per demand to prevent outages.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable the cluster auto-scaler on an existing cluster

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AKS should have outbound type set to user defined routing
recommendations.recommendation: AKS should have outbound type set to user defined routing

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configuring the user node pool with at least two nodes is essential for applications needing high availability, ensuring they remain operational and accessible without interruption.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure user nodepool count

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AKS Node Pools should have MaxSurge set
recommendations.recommendation: AKS Node Pools should have MaxSurge set

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AKS Cluster should be private
recommendations.recommendation: AKS Cluster should be private

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Availability Zones ensure high availability by offering independent locations within regions, equipped with their own power, cooling, and networking to ensure applications and data are protected from datacenter-level failures.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy AKS cluster across availability zones

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Monitor enables real-time health and performance insights for AKS by collecting events, capturing container logs, and gathering CPU/Memory data from the Metrics API. It allows data visualization using Azure Monitor Container Insights, Prometheus, Grafana, or others.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable AKS Monitoring

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AKS, popular for stateful apps needing backups, can now use Azure Backup to secure clusters and attached volumes through an installed Backup Extension, enabling backup and restore operations via a Backup Vault.
recommendations.recommendation: Back up Azure Kubernetes Service

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: The system node pool should be configured with a minimum node count of three to ensure critical system pods are resilient to node outages.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure system nodepool count

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AKS should have httpApplicationRouting disabled
recommendations.recommendation: AKS should have httpApplicationRouting disabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: From Kubernetes 1.26, Azure Disk and Azure File in-tree drivers are deprecated in favor of CSI drivers. Existing deployments remain operational but untested; users should switch to CSI drivers for new features and SKUs.
recommendations.recommendation: Upgrade Persistent Volumes using in-tree drivers to Azure CSI drivers

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AKS assigns the kubernetes.azure.com/mode: system label to nodes in system node pools signaling the preference for system pods should be scheduled there. The CriticalAddonsOnly=true:NoSchedule taint can be added to your system nodes to prohibit application pods from being scheduled on them.
recommendations.recommendation: Isolate system and application pods

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AKS should integrate authentication with AAD (Managed)
recommendations.recommendation: AKS should integrate authentication with AAD (Managed)

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure CNI enhances cluster IP and network management, allowing dynamic IP allocation, scalable subnets, direct pod-VNET connectivity, and supports diverse network policies for pods and nodes with Azure Network Policies and Calico, optimizing network efficiency and security
recommendations.recommendation: Configure Azure CNI networking for dynamic allocation of IPs or use CNI overlay

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Production AKS clusters require the Standard or Premium tier for a financially backed SLA and enhanced node scalability, as the free service lacks these features. Use the Premium tier for mission-critical workloads.
recommendations.recommendation: Update AKS tier to Standard or Premium
AKS Cluster should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Classic Application Insights retires in February 2024. To minimize disruption to existing application monitoring scenarios, transition to workspace-based Application Insights before 29 February 2024.
recommendations.recommendation: Convert Classic Deployments

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: ContainerApps should not allow insecure HTTP traffic. All ingress traffic should use HTTPS to ensure data is encrypted in transit.
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerApp should not allow insecure ingress traffic

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: ContainerApps should use Managed Identities to authenticate with Azure services instead of storing credentials in code or configuration.
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerApp should use Managed Identities

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: ContainerApps should use Azure Files for persistent storage to ensure data durability and availability across container restarts.
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerApp should use Azure Files to persist container data

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
ContainerApps should avoid using sticky sessions (session affinity)
as it can impact load distribution and high availability. Design applications
to be stateless when possible.
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerApp should avoid using session affinity

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Use SignalR with zone redundancy for production to improve uptime. This feature, available in the Premium tier, is activated upon creating or upgrading to Premium. Standard can upgrade to Premium without downtime.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable zone redundancy for SignalR
SignalR should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: SignalR should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: SignalR should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: ExpressRoute gateways are updated for improved functionality, reliability, performance, and security. Customer-controlled maintenance configuration and scheduling minimize update impact and align with your maintenance windows.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure customer-controlled ExpressRoute gateway maintenance

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Virtual Network Gateway should use zone-redundant SKUs (SKUs ending with "AZ") to provide high availability across availability zones.
recommendations.recommendation: Virtual Network Gateway should have availability zones enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: The active-active mode is available for all SKUs except Basic, allowing for two Gateway IP configurations and two public IP addresses, enhancing redundancy and traffic handling. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Active-Active VPN Gateways for redundancy

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: For improved reliability, each ExpressRoute gateway should connect to at least two circuits, with each circuit sourced from a different peering location. This setup ensures diverse connectivity paths, enhancing resilience and minimizing service disruption risks.
recommendations.recommendation: Connect ExpressRoute gateway with circuits from diverse peering locations

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Deploying zone-redundant virtual network gateways across availability zones ensures zone-resiliency, improving access to mission-critical, scalable services on Azure. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.
recommendations.recommendation: Choose a Zone-redundant VPN gateway
Virtual Network Gateway should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: For zone-redundant VPN gateways, always use zone-redundant Standard SKU public IPs to avoid deploying all instances in one zone. This ensures the gateway's reliability. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy VPN gateways with zone-redundant Public IPs

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Virtual Network Gateways without Point-to-site configuration or Connections.
recommendations.recommendation: Virtual Network Gateways without Point-to-site configuration or Connections

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: VPN gateways are updated for improved functionality, reliability, performance, and security. Customer-controlled maintenance configuration and scheduling minimize update impact and align with your maintenance windows.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure customer-controlled VPN gateway maintenance

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure ExpressRoute gateway offers variable SLAs based on deployment in single or multiple availability zones. To deploy virtual network gateways across zones automatically, use zone-redundant gateways for accessing critical, scalable services with increased resilience.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Zone-redundant ExpressRoute gateway SKUs

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: By default, Azure container registry requires authentication for pull/push actions. Enabling anonymous pull access exposes all content for public read actions. This applies to all repositories, potentially allowing unrestricted access if repository-scoped tokens are used.
recommendations.recommendation: Disable anonymous pull access

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Container Registry's optional zone redundancy enhances resiliency and high availability for registries or replication resources in a specific region by distributing resources across multiple zones.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable zone redundancy

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Enabling soft delete preview feature in Azure Container Registry (ACR) allows for the management of deleted artifacts with a specified retention period. Users can list, filter, and restore these artifacts until automatically purged post-retention.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable soft delete policy

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: ContainerRegistry should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerRegistry should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: ContainerRegistry should use retention policies
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerRegistry should use retention policies

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: ContainerRegistry should have the Administrator account disabled
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerRegistry should have the Administrator account disabled
ContainerRegistry should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Choose a service tier of Azure Container Registry to meet your performance needs. Premium offers the most bandwidth and highest rate of read and write operations for high-volume deployments. Use Basic to start, Standard for production, and Premium for hyper-scale performance and geo-replication.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Premium tier for critical production workloads

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Use Azure Container Registry's geo-replication for multi-region deployments to simplify registry management and minimize latency. It enables serving global customers from local data centers and supports distributed development teams. Regional webhooks can notify of events in replicas.
recommendations.recommendation: Create container registries with geo-replication enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Data Explorer Production Cluster should not use Dev SKU
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Data Explorer Production Cluster should not use Dev SKU

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Data Explorer should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Data Explorer should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Data Explorer should use Disk Encryption
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Data Explorer should use Disk Encryption

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Data Explorer should use Managed Identities
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Data Explorer should use Managed Identities
Azure Data Explorer should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: NAT Gateways not attached to any subnet.
recommendations.recommendation: NAT Gateways not attached to any subnet
NAT Gateway should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Logic App should limit access to Http Triggers
recommendations.recommendation: Logic App should limit access to Http Triggers
Logic App should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: APIM should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: APIM should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Upgrading the API Management instance to the Premium SKU adds support for Availability Zones, enhancing availability and resilience by distributing services across physically separate locations within Azure regions.
recommendations.recommendation: Migrate API Management services to Premium SKU to support Availability Zones
APIM should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: stv1 instances were deprecated on August 31, 2024. If not migrated to stv2 by then, auto-migration will occur. In some cases, due to technical limitations, services may be shut down in March 2025.
recommendations.recommendation: Azure API Management platform version should be stv2

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: APIM should use Managed Identities
recommendations.recommendation: APIM should use Managed Identities

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: APIM should only accept a minimum of TLS 1.2
recommendations.recommendation: APIM should only accept a minimum of TLS 1.2

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: APIM should should not accept weak or deprecated ciphers.
recommendations.recommendation: APIM should should not accept weak or deprecated ciphers.

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: APIM: Renew expiring certificates
recommendations.recommendation: APIM: Renew expiring certificates

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Zone redundancy for APIM instances ensures the gateway and control plane (Management API, developer portal, Git configuration) are replicated across datacenters in physically separated zones, boosting resilience to zone failures.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Availability Zones on Premium API Management instances

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Public IP addresses in Azure can be of standard SKU, available as non-zonal, zonal, or zone-redundant. Zone-redundant IPs are accessible across all zones, resisting any single zone failure, thereby providing higher resilience.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Standard SKU and Zone-Redundant IPs when applicable

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Prevent connectivity failures due to SNAT port exhaustion by employing NAT gateway for outbound traffic from virtual networks, ensuring dynamic scaling and secure internet connections.
recommendations.recommendation: Use NAT gateway for outbound connectivity to avoid SNAT Exhaustion

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Basic SKU public IP addresses will be retired on September 30, 2025. Users are advised to upgrade to Standard SKU public IP addresses before this date to avoid service disruptions.
recommendations.recommendation: Upgrade Basic SKU public IP addresses to Standard SKU

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Public IPs not attached to any resource.
recommendations.recommendation: Public IPs not attached to any resource

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Service Bus should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Service Bus should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Service Bus should have local authentication disabled
recommendations.recommendation: Service Bus should have local authentication disabled
Service Bus should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: As of 31 October 2024, TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 will no longer be supported on Azure including Service Bus to enhance security and provide best-in-class encryption for your data. Change the minimum TLS version for your Service Bus namespace to TLS v1.2 or higher.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure the minimum TLS version for Service Bus namespaces to TLS v1.2 or higher

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: SQL elastic pool without databases.
recommendations.recommendation: SQL elastic pool without databases

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: IP Groups not attached to any Azure Firewall.
recommendations.recommendation: IP Groups not attached to any Azure Firewall

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Service should have local authentication disabled
recommendations.recommendation: Service should have local authentication disabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Service should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Service should have private endpoints enabled
Service should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: DDoS protection without protected resources.
recommendations.recommendation: DDoS protection without protected resources

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Private DNS zones without Virtual Network Links.
recommendations.recommendation: Private DNS zones without Virtual Network Links

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Private DNS allows the Time-To-Live (TTL) for record sets in the zone to be set to a value between 1 and 2147483647 seconds. You should ensure that the TTL for the DNS record sets in your DNS Zones are set appropriately to meet your RTO targets.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure Time-To-Live (TTL) is set appropriately to ensure RTOs can be met

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: For most solutions, choose either Azure Front Door for content caching, CDN, TLS termination, and WAF, or Traffic Manager for simple global load balancing.
recommendations.recommendation: Avoid combining Traffic Manager and Front Door

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Monitor status should be online to ensure failover for application workload. If Traffic Manager's health shows Degraded, one or more endpoints may also be Degraded.
recommendations.recommendation: Traffic Manager Monitor Status Should be Online

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Traffic Manager without endpoints.
recommendations.recommendation: Traffic Manager without endpoints

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Traffic Manager: HTTP endpoints should be monitored using HTTPS
recommendations.recommendation: Traffic Manager: HTTP endpoints should be monitored using HTTPS
Traffic Manager should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: When configuring the Azure traffic manager, provision at least two endpoints to ensure workloads can fail-over to another instance, enhancing reliability and availability.
recommendations.recommendation: Traffic manager profiles should have more than one endpoint

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: For geographic routing, traffic is directed to endpoints based on specific regions. If a region fails, without a predefined failover, configuring an endpoint to "All (World)" for geographic profiles can prevent traffic black holes, ensuring service remains available.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure endpoint configured to (All World) for geographic profiles

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Set up monitoring and alerts for ExpressRoute Gateway. Create alert rule for ensuring promptly response to critical events such as exceeding packets per second, exceeding BGP routes prefixes, Gateway overutilization and high frequency in route changes.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor health for ExpressRoute gateway

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Avoid using ExpressRoute circuits for VNet to VNet communication. Use Virtual WAN to connect VNets in different regions. This feature is enabled at the ExpressRoute circuit level but reflected in the Virtual WAN resource.
recommendations.recommendation: Avoid using ExpressRoute circuits for VNet to VNet communication

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Database for MySQL - Flexible Server should have private access enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Database for MySQL - Flexible Server should have private access enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Enable HA with zone redundancy on flexible server instances to deploy a standby replica in a different zone, offering automatic failover capability for improved reliability and disaster recovery.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable HA with zone redundancy

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configure GRS to ensure that your database meets its availability and durability targets even in the face of failures or disasters.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure geo redundant backup storage
Azure Database for MySQL - Flexible Server should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configure one or more read replicas to ensure that your database meets its availability and durability targets even in the face of failures or disasters.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure one or more read replicas

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configure storage auto-grow to prevent the server from running out of storage and becoming read-only.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure storage auto-grow

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Network Watcher offers tools for monitoring, diagnosing, viewing metrics, and managing logs for IaaS resources. It helps maintain the health of VMs, VNets, application gateways, load balancers, but not for PaaS or Web analytics.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy Network Watcher in all regions where you have networking services

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Improves monitoring for Azure and Hybrid connectivity
recommendations.recommendation: Configure Network Watcher Connection monitor

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Set up monitoring and alerts for Point-to-Site VPN gateways. Create alert rule for ensuring promptly response to critical events such as Gateway over utilization, connection count limits and User VPN route limits. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor health for v-Hub's Point-to-Site VPN gateways

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Resource should comply with naming conventions
recommendations.recommendation: Resource should comply with naming conventions

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Resource should have tags applied for proper resource management, cost tracking, and organization. Tags help categorize resources and provide metadata for governance and operational purposes.
recommendations.recommendation: Resource should have tags

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Resource Groups without resources.
recommendations.recommendation: Resource Groups without resources

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: SQL servers should enforce TLS 1.2 or higher to ensure secure encrypted connections to the database.
recommendations.recommendation: SQL should enforce TLS >= 1.2

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: SQL servers should have private endpoints enabled to secure database access and prevent exposure to the public internet.
recommendations.recommendation: SQL should have private endpoints enabled
Plan should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: App Service should be configured with a minimum of two instances for production workloads. If apps have a longer warmup time a minimum of three instances should be used.
recommendations.recommendation: Set minimum instance count to 2 for app service

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: App Service plans without hosting Apps.
recommendations.recommendation: App Service plans without hosting Apps

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure's feature of deploying App Service plans across availability zones enhances resiliency and reliability by ensuring operation during datacenter failures, providing redundancy without needing different regions, thus minimizing downtime and maintaining uninterrupted services.
recommendations.recommendation: Migrate App Service to availability Zone Support

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Choose Standard/Premium Azure App Service Plan for robust apps with advanced scaling, high availability, better performance, and multiple slots, ensuring resilience and continuous operation.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Standard or Premium tier

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Device Provisioning Service (DPS) enables easy redistribution of IoT devices for scaling and availability, allowing devices to be reassigned and not bound to specific IoT Hub instances. Devices in IoT Hubs using DPS should be verified for DPS utilization.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Device Provisioning Service

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Using message routing for custom endpoints in IoT Hub, messages might not reach these destinations if specific conditions are unmet. A default route ensures all messages are received, but disabling this safety net risks leaving some messages undelivered.
recommendations.recommendation: Disabled Fallback Route

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: In a production scenario, the IoT Hub tier should not be Free because the Free tier does not provide the necessary Service Level Agreement.
recommendations.recommendation: Do not use free tier

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Cache for Redis offers zone redundancy in Premium and Enterprise tiers, using VMs across multiple Availability Zones to ensure greater resilience and availability.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable zone redundancy for Azure Cache for Redis

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Redis should not enable non SSL ports
recommendations.recommendation: Redis should not enable non SSL ports

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Redis should enforce TLS >= 1.2
recommendations.recommendation: Redis should enforce TLS >= 1.2
Redis should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Create up to two maintenance windows for the Azure Virtual Desktop agent, side-by-side stack, and Geneva Monitoring agent to get updated so that updates don't happen during peak business hours.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure host pool scheduled agent updates

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Availability Sets not associated to any VM or VMSS.
recommendations.recommendation: Availability Sets not associated to any VM or VMSS

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Event Hub Namespace should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Event Hub Namespace should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Event Hub should have local authentication disabled
recommendations.recommendation: Event Hub should have local authentication disabled
Event Hub Namespace should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: When using the Azure portal, zone redundancy is automatically enabled. However, some Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools may default this to false. To ensure replication of metadata and events across data centers in an availability zone, always verify that zone redundancy is enabled.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure zone redundancy is enabled in supported regions

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Enable auto-inflate on Event Hub Standard tier namespaces to automatically scale up throughput units (TUs), meeting usage needs and preventing data ingress or egress throttle scenarios by adjusting to allowed rates.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable auto-inflate on Event Hub Standard tier

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: By default, Azure SQL Database premium tier provisions multiple copies within the same region. For geo redundancy, databases can be set as Zone Redundant, distributing copies across Azure Availability Zones to maintain availability during regional outages.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable zone redundancy for Azure SQL Database to achieve high availability and resiliency
SQL Database should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Synapse Workspace should have managed virtual network enabled to establish proper network segmentation boundaries.
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Synapse Workspace should establish network segmentation boundaries

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Synapse Workspace should have public network access disabled to prevent unauthorized access from the internet.
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Synapse Workspace should disable public network access

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Synapse Workspace should have private endpoints configured to secure access and prevent exposure to the public internet.
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Synapse Workspace should have private endpoints enabled
Azure Synapse Workspace should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Use custom autoscale for VMSS based on metrics and schedules to improve performance and cost effectiveness, adjusting instances as demand changes.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure VMSS Autoscale to custom and configure the scaling metrics

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: When creating VMSS, implement availability zones as a protection measure for your applications and data against the rare event of datacenter failure.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy VMSS across availability zones with VMSS Flex

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Enabling automatic instance repairs in Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets enhances application availability through a continuous health check and maintenance process.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Automatic Repair Policy on Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Monitoring application health in Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets is crucial for deployment management. It supports rolling upgrades such as automatic OS-image upgrades and VM guest patching, leveraging health monitoring for upgrading.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Azure Virtual Machine Scale Set Application Health Monitoring

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Deploying even single instance VMs into a scale set with Flexible orchestration mode future-proofs applications for scaling and availability. This mode guarantees high availability (up to 1000 VMs) by distributing VMs across fault domains in a region or within an Availability Zone.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy VMSS with Flex orchestration mode instead of Uniform

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Predictive autoscale utilizes machine learning to efficiently manage and scale Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets by forecasting CPU load through historical usage analysis, ensuring timely scale-out to meet demand.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Predictive autoscale and configure at least for Forecast Only

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Ephemeral OS disks on AKS offer lower read/write latency due to local attachment, eliminating the need for replication seen with managed disks. This enhances performance and speeds up cluster operations such as scaling or upgrading due to quicker re-imaging and boot times.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Ephemeral OS Disks for AKS VMSS Node Pools

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Microsoft advises disabling strictly even VM instance distribution across Availability Zones in VMSS to improve scalability and flexibility, noting that uneven distribution may better serve application load demands despite the potential trade-off in resilience.
recommendations.recommendation: Disable Force strictly even balance across zones to avoid scale in and out fail attempts

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Set up monitoring and alerts for v-Hub's VPN Gateway. Create alert rule for ensuring promptly response to critical events such as packet drop counts, BGP status, Gateway over utilization. Mission Critical workloads should use dual ExpressRoutes instead of VPN.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor gateway for Site-to-site v-Hub's VPN gateway

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Monitor Azure Firewall for overall health, processed throughput, and outbound SNAT port usage. Get alerted before limits impact services. Consider NAT gateway integration with zonal deployments; note limitations with zone redundant firewalls and secure virtual hub networks.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor Azure Firewall metrics

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Firewall offers different SLAs depending on its deployment; in a single availability zone or across multiple, potentially improving reliability and performance.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy Azure Firewall across multiple availability zones
Azure Firewall should have diagnostic settings enabled
Azure Analysis Service should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Rate limiting controls traffic volume between on-premises networks and Azure via ExpressRoute Direct, applying to private or Microsoft peering. It distributes port bandwidth, ensures stability, and prevents congestion, with steps outlined for enabling on circuits.
recommendations.recommendation: Implement rate-limiting across ExpressRoute Direct Circuits to optimize network flow
Azure Data Factory should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: ExpressRoute gateways facilitate network traffic and route exchanges. FastPath enhances on-premises to virtual network data path performance by directing traffic straight to virtual machines, bypassing the gateway for improved resiliency through reduced gateway utilization.
recommendations.recommendation: For better data path performance enable FastPath on ExpressRoute Connections
Event Grid Domain should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Event Grid Domain should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Event Grid Domain should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Event Grid Domain should have local authentication disabled
recommendations.recommendation: Event Grid Domain should have local authentication disabled
Key Vault should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Key Vault's soft-delete feature enables recovery of deleted vaults and objects like keys, secrets, and certificates. When enabled, marked resources are retained for 90 days, allowing for their recovery, essentially undoing deletion.
recommendations.recommendation: Key vaults should have soft delete enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Purge protection secures against malicious deletions by enforcing a retention period for soft deleted key vaults, ensuring no one, not even insiders or Microsoft, can purge your key vaults during this period, preventing permanent data loss.
recommendations.recommendation: Key vaults should have purge protection enabled
AppConfiguration should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AppConfiguration should have local authentication disabled
recommendations.recommendation: AppConfiguration should have local authentication disabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: With Purge protection enabled, soft deleted stores can't be purged in the retention period. If disabled, the soft deleted store can be purged before the retention period expires.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Purge protection for Azure App Configuration

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: SLA is not available for Free tier. Upgrade to the Standard tier to get an SLA of 99.9%
recommendations.recommendation: Upgrade to App Configuration Standard tier

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: AppConfiguration should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: AppConfiguration should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Expired certificates.
recommendations.recommendation: Expired certificates

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
Fabric Capacity should have administrators configured to ensure
proper management and security oversight of the capacity.
recommendations.recommendation: Fabric Capacity should have administrators configured

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
Fabric Capacity should use the Fabric (F) SKU tier for production
workloads to ensure proper performance and support levels.
recommendations.recommendation: Fabric Capacity should use Fabric (F) SKU tier for production workloads

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
Fabric Capacity should be in Active state to be available for
use. Paused capacities are not available for processing workloads.
recommendations.recommendation: Fabric Capacity should be in Active state

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Redundancy ensures storage accounts meet availability and durability targets amidst failures, weighing lower costs against higher availability. Locally redundant storage offers the least durability at the lowest cost.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure that storage accounts are zone or region redundant
Storage should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Storage Account should require secure transfer (HTTPS) to ensure all data transfers are encrypted in transit.
recommendations.recommendation: Storage Account should use HTTPS only

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Storage Account should enforce minimum TLS version 1.2 to ensure secure communications using modern encryption protocols.
recommendations.recommendation: Storage Account should enforce TLS >= 1.2

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Storage Account should have immutable storage with versioning enabled to protect against accidental or malicious data deletion or modification.
recommendations.recommendation: Storage Account should have immutable storage versioning enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: General-purpose v2 accounts are recommended for most storage scenarios offering the latest features or the lowest per-gigabyte pricing. Legacy accounts like Standard general-purpose v1 and Blob Storage aren't advised by Microsoft but may fit specific scenarios.
recommendations.recommendation: Consider upgrading legacy storage accounts to v2 storage accounts

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Improves monitoring and security for Azure and Hybrid connectivity
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Virtual Network Flow Logs

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Virtual Networks without subnets.
recommendations.recommendation: Virtual Networks without subnets

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Virtual Network should have at least two DNS servers assigned
recommendations.recommendation: Virtual Network should have at least two DNS servers assigned
Virtual Network should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server is on the retirement path
recommendations.recommendation: Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server is on the retirement path
Azure Database for MySQL - Single Server should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Create Alerts with Azure Monitor for operations like creating or updating Network Security Group rules to catch unauthorized/undesired changes to resources and spot attempts to bypass firewalls or access resources from the outside.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor changes in Network Security Groups with Azure Monitor

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Network Security Groups not attached to any network interface or subnet.
recommendations.recommendation: Network Security Groups not attached to any network interface or subnet
NSG should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Stream Analytics cluster (dedicated) offers more reliable performance guarantees. All the jobs running on your cluster belong only to you. You can also have access to important features like private endpoints, Auto-Scaling, Vnet Support, etc.
recommendations.recommendation: Run jobs in your own dedicated Stream Analytics cluster for increased reliability and security

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Configure Autoscale to allow your job to dynamically change the allocated number of Streaming Units (SU) based on load, metrics, and/or schedule.
recommendations.recommendation: Migrate Stream Analytics jobs to StandardV2 SKU

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: To take advantage of availability zones, you must enable zone redundancy when you create a Container Apps environment. The environment must include a virtual network with an available subnet. To ensure proper distribution of replicas, set your app's minimum replica count to three.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy zone redundant Container app environments

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Container Apps Environment should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: Container Apps Environment should have private endpoints enabled
Container Apps Environment should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
Azure Container Instances should use private IP addresses instead
of public IPs for enhanced security and to follow network security best practices.
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerInstance should use private IP addresses

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
Azure Container Instances should have availability zones enabled
to ensure high availability and protect against datacenter failures.
recommendations.recommendation: ContainerInstance should have availability zones enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Provisioning ExpressRoute circuits on a 10-Gbps or 100-Gbps ExpressRoute Direct resource up to 20-Gbps or 200-Gbps is possible but not recommended for resiliency. If an ExpressRoute Direct port fails, and circuits are using full capacity, the remaining port won't handle the extra load.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure ExpressRoute Direct is not over-subscribed

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: In Azure ExpressRoute Direct, the "Admin State" indicates the administrative status of layer 1 links, showing if a link is enabled or disabled, effectively turning the physical port on or off.
recommendations.recommendation: The Admin State of both Links of an ExpressRoute Direct should be in Enabled state

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Front Door WAF Policy without associations.
recommendations.recommendation: Front Door WAF Policy without associations

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Application Gateways without backend targets.
recommendations.recommendation: Application Gateways without backend targets

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Deploying Application Gateway in a zone-aware configuration ensures continued customer access to services even if a specific zone goes down, as services in other zones remain available.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy Application Gateway in a zone-redundant configuration

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Azure Application Gateways v2 are always deployed in a highly available fashion with multiple instances by default. Enabling autoscale ensures the service is not reliant on manual intervention for scaling.
recommendations.recommendation: Ensure Autoscale feature has been enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Use Application Gateway v2 for built-in features like autoscaling, static VIPs, Azure KeyVault integration for better traffic management and performance, unless v1 is necessary.
recommendations.recommendation: Migrate to Application Gateway v2

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Using custom health probes enhances understanding of backend availability and facilitates monitoring of backend services for any impact.
recommendations.recommendation: Use Health Probes to detect backend availability
Application Gateway: Monitor and Log the configurations and traffic

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Using connection draining for backend maintenance ensures graceful removal of backend pool members during updates or health issues. It's enabled via Backend Setting and applies to all members during rule creation.
recommendations.recommendation: Plan for backend maintenance by using connection draining

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Application Gateway (Standard_v2 or WAF_v2 SKU) can support up to 125 instances (125 instance IP addresses + 1 private frontend IP configuration + 5 Azure reserved). A minimum subnet size of /24 is recommended.
recommendations.recommendation: A minimum subnet size of /24 is recommended for Application Gateway v2 subnets.

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: CosmosDB should have private endpoints enabled
recommendations.recommendation: CosmosDB should have private endpoints enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: CosmosDB: disable write operations on metadata resources (databases, containers, throughput) via account keys
recommendations.recommendation: CosmosDB: disable write operations on metadata resources (databases, containers, throughput) via account keys

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: CosmosDB should have local authentication disabled
recommendations.recommendation: CosmosDB should have local authentication disabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Cosmos DB's backup is always on, offering protection against data mishaps. Continuous mode allows for self-serve restoration to a pre-mishap point, unlike periodic mode which requires contacting Microsoft support, leading to longer restore times.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure continuous backup mode

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: When availability zones are configured, Azure Cosmos DB intelligently distributes the 4 replicas of your data across all available zones. It ensures that your Azure Cosmos DB can withstand an outage in one availability zone and remain fully operational throughout.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable availability zones

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Cosmos DB boasts high uptime and resiliency. Even so, issues may arise. With Service-Managed failover, if a region is down, Cosmos DB automatically switches to the next available region, requiring no user action.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable service-managed failover for multi-region accounts with single write region

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Multi-region writes enable high availability but require careful consistency and conflict resolution. Using Bounded Staleness is an anti-pattern, as replication lag increases latency and coordination overhead. This setup undermines scalability, impacting performance and availability.
recommendations.recommendation: Evaluate multi-region write capability

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Enable a secondary region in Cosmos DB for higher SLA without downtime. Simple as pinning a location on a map. For Strong consistency, configure at least three regions for write availability in case of failure.
recommendations.recommendation: Configure at least two regions for high availability
CosmosDB should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance:
To improve the resiliency of a Microsoft Entra Domain Services managed domain, or deploy to additional geographic locations close to your applications, you can use replica sets.
You can add a replica set to any peered virtual network in any Azure region that supports Domain Services.
recommendations.recommendation: Use replica sets for resiliency or geolocation in Microsoft Entra Domain Services

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: You need to use a minimum of Enterprise SKU for your managed domain to support replica sets.
recommendations.recommendation: Use at least the Enterprise SKU

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: The Azure Image Builder service lacks availability zones support. Replicating Image Templates to a secondary region will enable the build of new images in secondary region.
recommendations.recommendation: Replicate your Image Templates to a secondary region

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: A private endpoint has two custom properties, static IP address and the network interface name, which must be set at creation. If not in Succeeded state, there may be issues with the endpoint or associated resource.
recommendations.recommendation: Resolve issues with Private Endpoints in non Succeeded connection state

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Private Endpoints not connected to any resource.
recommendations.recommendation: Private Endpoints not connected to any resource

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Basic SKU is not recommended for critical workloads. Standard SKU provides important features Inter-hub and VNet-to-VNet transiting through the virtual hub, ExpressRoute, VPN and Point-to-Site Gateways, ability to deploy Azure Firewalls and NVAs.
recommendations.recommendation: Migrate from Basic to Standard Virtual WAN

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Set up monitoring and alerts for v-Hubs. Create alert rule for ensuring promptly response to changes in BGP status and Data processed by v-Hubs.
recommendations.recommendation: Monitor health for v-Hubs
Azure FrontDoor should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Logic App should enforce HTTPS-only traffic to ensure all communications are encrypted and secure.
recommendations.recommendation: Logic App should use HTTPS only

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: App Service should use virtual network integration to securely access resources in Azure virtual networks.
recommendations.recommendation: App Service should use VNET integration

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Use Health Check for production workloads. Health check increases your application's availability by rerouting requests away from unhealthy instances, and replacing instances if they remain unhealthy. The Health check path should check critical components of your application.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable Health check for App Services

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Logic App should disable Client Affinity (ARR Affinity) for better scalability and reliability.
recommendations.recommendation: Logic App should avoid using Client Affinity

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Create a deployment slot for staging to deploy updates, verify them, and ensure all instances are warmed up before production swap, reducing bad update chances. An LKG slot allows easy rollback to a previous good deployment if issues arise later, enhancing reliability.
recommendations.recommendation: Deploy to a staging slot

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Function App should have VNET Route All enabled to ensure all outbound traffic goes through the integrated virtual network.
recommendations.recommendation: Function should have VNET Route all enabled for VNET integration

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Logic App should use virtual network integration to securely access resources in Azure virtual networks.
recommendations.recommendation: Logic App should use VNET integration

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Auto Heal allows you to mitigate your apps when it runs into unexpected situations like HTTP server errors, resource exhaustion, etc. You can configure different triggers based on your need and choose to recycle the app to recover it from a bad state.
recommendations.recommendation: Enable auto heal for Functions App

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Function App should enforce HTTPS-only traffic to ensure all communications are encrypted and secure.
recommendations.recommendation: Function should use HTTPS only

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Logic App should have VNET Route All enabled to ensure all outbound traffic goes through the integrated virtual network.
recommendations.recommendation: Logic App should have VNET Route all enabled for VNET integration

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: App Service should disable Client Affinity (ARR Affinity) for better scalability and reliability. Client affinity can cause session stickiness issues in multi-instance deployments.
recommendations.recommendation: App Service should avoid using Client Affinity

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: App Service should have VNET Route All enabled to ensure all outbound traffic goes through the integrated virtual network.
recommendations.recommendation: App Service should have VNET Route all enabled for VNET integration
App should have diagnostic settings enabled

recommendations.bestPracticesGuidance: Use app settings for configuration and define them in Resource Manager templates or via PowerShell to facilitate part of an automated deployment/update process for improved reliability.
recommendations.recommendation: Store configuration as app settings for Web Sites

recommendations.recommendation: Function should use VNET integration
